description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, flared brass horn attached to a wooden base. The base is rectangular, crafted from polished wood with decorative trim and a small latch on the front, suggesting a compartment or storage area. The horn is ornate, with a scalloped edge and a yellowish-gold metallic finish, featuring subtle decorative perforations. On top of the wooden base sits a black turntable platter with a visible red label at its center, and a gold-colored tonearm resting on its side. A hand-crank mechanism is attached to the right side of the base, indicating manual operation. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs, blending wood and metal for a classic, elegant appearance.
description: A vintage wooden gramophone with a large brass horn, black turntable platter, and hand-crank mechanism, featuring ornate details and a polished finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden base with a latch, the black turntable platter, and the large brass horn curving upward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full face of the horn, the hand-crank on the right, and the decorative side panel of the wooden base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the horn extending leftward, showing the tonearm and the smooth wooden base.
description_view_3: Rear view with the horn's opening facing forward, the hand-crank visible on the left, and the back panel of the wooden base.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and centrally located on the wooden base, the turntable platter is circular, black, and features a red label at its center. It appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish and no visible grooves or patterns. The platter is proportionally smaller than the base, occupying the top surface without overhanging.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, gold-colored, and appears to be made of metal. It is curved and rests to the right of the platter, with a visible pivot point near its base. The tonearm is relatively short and positioned in a resting state, not over the platter.
